
For this project, my research question was does college education have an impact on your education and whether there are any patterns/correlations within my chosen data sets. I got my datasets from github (below are the links to the datasets).

Data Sets:
tuition_cost - https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2020/2020-03-10/tuition_cost.csv

student_diversity - https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2020/2020-03-10/student_diversity.csv

salary_potential - https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2020/2020-03-10/salary_potential.csv

I first started with combining all these dataset together and compiling top universities and bottom universities in terms of mid-career salary. I also wanted to do early-career salaries but unfortunately I ran out of time to do so (Might be due to possible ADHD but unconfirmed! Will get diagnosed soon! So excited!). Below is my generated graph for this:

Mid Career.svg…]()

I only used the top 5 and bottom 5 universities for comparisons due to the fact that adding more will mess up the graph clearity and I did not wanna deal with the mess of attempting to adjust for it. You can see that attending certain colleges do lead to higher salaries. I wish I could find a data set for employed individuals that also includes what education level they have. This unfortunately does not answer my question on whether having a college education impacts salaries. It does prove that depending on which college you attend, your forecasted salary can be drastically different. I did find interesting data regarding gender pay and race pay.

With these above figures, you can see that on average men get paid more than women in both early career and mid career. You can also see that on average white americans get paid more than minorities. I got these by filtering for women dominated school and men dominated schools and then comparing the average salaries of these schools. I did the same with the race salary comparison. This is in no way a perfect comparison as simply comparing women dominated schools vs men dominated schools or minority dominated school is not a good metric to use to compare salaries. It does somewhat show that despite all these individuals having college educations, they all are paid differently. 

Overall, we can see that there are different salary levels depending on the college you attend as well as men and white americans being paid more than women and minority groups. I will say that there are many confounding variables such as what degrees people had, what level of college education do they have, as well as my approach not being the most ideal approach to draw conclusions.
